title: Cyathella alata
intro: Cyathella alata, also known as the alpine saxifrage, is a perennial plant species belonging to the Saxifragaceae family, commonly found in alpine and subalpine regions. Traditionally, the plant has been used in folk medicine to treat various ailments, with reported health benefits including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of digestive issues and skin conditions. The bioactive constituents of Cyathella alata include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The plant is commonly used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, often in combination with other herbs, to support digestive health and immune function.
taxonomy: Cyathella alata is classified as a genus of liverworts, specifically within the family Cyathellaceae. It belongs to the class Marchantiopsida and the phylum Marchantiophyta. This plant is further categorized under the division Bryophyta and the kingdom Plantae. Cyathella alata is native to certain regions, and its classification is closely related to its unique characteristics and habitats. As a liverwort, it plays a significant role in the ecosystem, contributing to the diversity of plant life in its native range.
